metadata.title: Nora Soini – Therapist & Craniosacral Therapy Espoo
metadata.description: Short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches and craniosacral therapy student, based in Espoo.

hero.heading: Gently by your side through change
hero.description: Clarity, peace and connection to yourself.

situations.heading: Situations I can help with
situations.description: Every situation is unique. You are welcome to come, for example, when:

cards.title: Stress and exhaustion
cards.description: You feel stressed, burnt out, or your nervous system is overactivated and recovery feels hard.

cards.title: Life transitions
cards.description: You are at a crossroads or looking for clarity about your direction.

cards.title: Connection to self
cards.description: You long for a deeper connection to yourself, your feelings and your body's wisdom.

cards.title: Lingering tension
cards.description: You carry experiences or tensions that still affect your everyday life.

about.quote: "I believe every person already holds within them a direction towards balance."
about.heading: Gently by your side through change
about.description: I am a short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches and I am training to become a craniosacral therapist (graduating autumn 2026).
about.description2: What matters most in my work is a safe, calm space where a person can be met as a whole – body, emotions and mind.
about.description3: My path to this work has run through a long journey of personal growth. My own experiences have taught me a great deal about humanity and how a person can find their way back to connection with themselves.

pillars.heading: Three cornerstones of my practice
pillars.description: I combine conversation, presence and somatic listening to create a holistic and safe space.

items.title: Conversation
items.description: Together we explore feelings, bodily experiences and the relationships and life history that shape you – in a genuine, safe encounter.

items.title: Presence
items.description: A moment where you get to be seen and heard exactly as you are – without hurry or demands.

items.title: Body awareness
items.description: Craniosacral therapy calms the nervous system through gentle touch. The body holds wisdom that words cannot always reach.

process.heading: How to get started
process.description: The first step of reaching out can feel big – that is why I want to make it as easy as possible.

steps.title: Free introductory call
steps.description: A short, non-committal 15-minute call. You can share your situation and ask me anything.

steps.title: Book a first appointment
steps.description: You can also book directly. At the first session we will calmly go through your situation and wishes.

steps.title: Send a message
steps.description: Not sure which option suits you? Send me a message and I will help you find the right way to start.

faq.heading: Answers to common questions
faq.description: It is completely normal for therapy or a new treatment to raise questions. You can also reach me at nora@norasoini.fi

items.question: How do I know if I need therapy?
items.answer: If something is weighing on your mind or your life situation feels burdensome, therapy can help. You don't need a 'big enough' problem. The first meeting is a free introduction.

items.question: Is therapy confidential?
items.answer: Yes. Everything discussed in therapy sessions is confidential. I am bound by professional secrecy.

items.question: How long does therapy last?
items.answer: Short-term therapy is typically 5–20 sessions. The length of the process is planned individually according to your needs.

items.question: How much does therapy cost?
items.answer: Short-term therapy sessions are €89 / 60 min. Short-term therapy is not currently covered by Kela reimbursement, but it is worth checking with your employer or insurance company.

items.question: Can sessions be arranged remotely?
items.answer: Yes, short-term therapy sessions can be arranged remotely via video call. Craniosacral therapy is always in Espoo.

items.question: Do I need to know anything in particular?
items.answer: Nothing in particular. Come as you are.

items.question: What is craniosacral therapy?
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy is a gentle and calm treatment in which the body is listened to through light touch. The aim is to release tensions, support nervous system balance and promote the body's own recovery.

items.question: What does a craniosacral therapy session involve?
items.answer: The treatment is carried out fully clothed on a therapy table. Touch is very light and the session progresses calmly, listening to the client's body. Many people find the treatment deeply relaxing.

items.question: What can craniosacral therapy help with?
items.answer: People come for craniosacral therapy when there is stress, anxiety or chronic tension in body or mind. It can help you pause, calm down and strengthen your connection to your own body.

items.question: What is LST therapy based on?
items.answer: Presence and Systemic Therapy (LST) combines several therapeutic approaches: family constellation, emotion-focused therapy (EFT), ACT (acceptance and commitment therapy), solution-focused therapy and mindfulness. The person is seen as a whole.

items.question: Is craniosacral therapy covered by insurance?
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy does not replace a doctor's examination or other healthcare services, and no medical diagnosis is made. The treatment is not covered by Kela reimbursement.

metadata.title: Services – Nora Soini
metadata.description: Short-term therapy and craniosacral therapy in Espoo and online. I also offer combined holistic sessions.

hero.heading: Short-term therapy and craniosacral therapy in Espoo and online

services.cardQuote: You are welcome to reach out – I will help you find the right option for you.

items.title: Craniosacral therapy
items.subtitle: Espoo · 60 min · Student rate €69 (→ €95 from 1 Sep 2026)

metadata.title: Nora Soini – About the therapist
metadata.description: Short-term therapist and craniosacral therapy student based in Espoo.

hero.heading: I am a short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches – and I am training to become a craniosacral therapist.

intro.heading: My path to this work
paragraphs: I am in my early sixties, a mother of two grown-up children, and I have been married for a long time. In 2011 I became an entrepreneur – before that I worked for many years in an international company.
Through my own therapy journey I discovered the healer within myself years ago. When your own inner work goes deep enough, a desire often arises to also be of support to others on their journey.

Along the way I have faced profound challenges. As a teenager my parents divorced; as a young adult I was abandoned without explanation; and as an adult I was subjected to prolonged bullying. These experiences left their mark, but they have also taught me a great deal about people, vulnerability and how a person can find their way back to connection with themselves.
Today I see those experiences as some of the greatest learning moments of my life. They have cultivated gratitude, compassion, sensitivity and the ability to meet another person genuinely.

What matters most in my work is a safe, calm space where a person can be met as a whole – body, emotions and mind. I believe every person already holds within them a direction towards balance and wholeness.
